answer:
Higgsfield MCP is a tool surface that integrates over 30 image and video generation models into any Model Context Protocol-compatible AI agent, allowing for direct creation of visual content within a chat interface [1][2][3].

The primary, official method is to use their hosted server at `https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp` [1][2][4]. This method uses OAuth authentication via your Higgsfield account, requiring no manual API keys or local installation [1][5][4].

For most clients (e.g., Claude Desktop, Cursor), you add the URL to your configuration or settings, authenticate through your browser, and gain access to tools like generate_image and generate_video [1][2][4][3]. For Claude Code, you can use the terminal command:

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http --scope user higgsfield https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp
```

While some third-party or older local implementations involve manual Python environments and API key management [6], the official Higgsfield-maintained approach is the recommended, simplified hosted path [4][3][7].
